title: Int5/Int6+Zstd+MLP3x: mean val_bpb=1.1752 (10L, seq4096, sliding window)
author: shajalahamedcse
body:
## Int5/Int6 Mixed Quantization + Zstd + 3x MLP Expansion

**Mean val_bpb: 1.1752** (3 seeds, std=0.0017)

| Seed | val_bpb | Artifact |
|------|---------|----------|
| 1337 | 1.1773 | 15,708,798 B |
| 42   | 1.1732 | 15,700,116 B |
| 7    | 1.1750 | 15,711,843 B |
| **mean** | **1.1752** | |
| **std** | **0.0017** | |

## Key techniques

1. **Int5 quantization for MLP matrices** — 5-bit packing frees ~1.5MB, enabling 3x MLP expansion
2. **Int6 quantization for attention matrices** — balances precision and size
3. **Zstd compression** — better ratio than zlib on quantized integer arrays
4. **MLP expansion 3x** (hidden=1536 vs baseline 1024) — funded by int5 savings
5. **Seq4096 training + sliding window eval** (stride=64) — full context scoring

## Hardware

Modal 8×H100 SXM, `torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=8`, 600s cap
submission_readme:
# Seq4096 Training + 10L + Sliding Window Eval

## Result

| Seed | val_bpb | Artifact |
|------|---------|----------|
| 1337 | 1.1900 | 15,115,793 B |
| 42   | 1.1908 | 15,128,724 B |
| 7    | 1.1888 | 15,154,068 B |
| **mean** | **1.1899** | |
| **std**  | **0.0008** | |

## Key change

Train on **4096-token sequences** instead of 1024. The model sees longer
dependencies during training, improving predictions. All other settings
follow the sliding window SOTA baseline (10 layers, eval_stride=64,
FP16 embeddings, Muon + weight decay, Overtone init).

## Hyperparameters

```
num_layers     = 10
train_seq_len  = 4096
eval_stride    = 64
warmdown_iters = 3600
matrix_lr      = 0.04  (default)
muon_momentum  = 0.95  (default)
```

## Hardware

Modal 8×H100 SXM, `torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=8`
Training capped at 600 seconds (`MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=600`).
